rulings.Case Title: GREENMD, LLC ET AL VS. NGUEY LAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Nguey Lay Notice Of Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 12, 2019, Line 3, Defendant Nguey Lay's Notice Of Motion To Quash. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Moving party and party issuing subpoena have filed Joint Stipulation and (Proposed) Order Limiting Deposition Subpoena for Production of Records re San Francisco Office of Cannabis and their being no opposition file by any other party to the action, the Stipulation is approved and will be so ordered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUU TRAN ET AL VS. JAVELIN LOGISTICS COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS WINSPEAR LOGISTICS CORPORATION AND MALCOLM WINSPEAR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Winspear Logistics Corporation and Malcolm Winspear's demurrer to complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs have 20 days leave to amend to allege alter ego liability. The court previously overruled defendant Javelin Logistics Company, Inc.'s demurrer because plaintiff alleges facts regarding successor liability. (First Amended Complaint, Paras. 31-34 and 38; McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Ass'n, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 753 [successor corporation can be liable where "the transaction amounts to a consolidation or merger of the two corporations" or "the purchasing corporation is merely a continuation of the selling corporation."].) The focus of this demurrer is whether plaintiffs' alter ego allegations are sufficient. Successor liability and alter ego liability are distinct equitable concepts. (See Brown Bark III, L.P. v. Haver (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 809, 823 ["Although the showing required to invoke these two equitable doctrines is different, their effect is the same."].) Plaintiffs have leave to amend to allege ultimate facts showing that defendants Winspear Logistics and Malcom Winspear are alter egos of each other. (See Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 236 [plaintiff sufficiently pled alter ego theory of liability where it alleged that "Caswell dominated and controlled PDR; that a unity of interest and ownership existed between Caswell and PDR; that PDR was a mere shell and conduit for Caswell's affairs; that PDR was inadequately capitalized; that PDR failed to abide by the formalities of corporate existence; that Caswell used PDR assets as her own; and that recognizing the separate existence of PDR would promote injustice."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S Motion For Order Granting Prior Separate Trial Of Special Defenses. Off calendar. Defendant failed to serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code Civ. Proc., § 1005(b). The Court further notes that even if the statute of limitations issue had not already been decided by its order overruling defendant's demurrer, the court would deny any motion for a separate trial without prejudice to its renewal before the assigned trial judge. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA O'BRIEN VS. WEST PORTAL ORAL AND FACIAL SURGERY CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT WEST PORTAL ORAL AND FACIAL SURGERY CENTER'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. Defendant West Portal Oral and Facial Surgery Center's motion to compel arbitration is granted, and the action is stayed pending the completion of arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc., § 1281.4.) Based on plaintiff's opposition, there is no dispute that the parties are subject to a binding arbitration agreement. The court encourages the parties to mediate their dispute, but it lacks the authority to grant plaintiff's request to compel mediation. "Essential to the mediation process is the concept that the parties are in control of resolving their own dispute." (Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, 540.) Mediation is voluntary. (Jen-Weld, 146 Cal.App.4th at 541.) To that end, the court cannot force parties to mediate because an order compelling mediation is antithetical to the entire concept of mediation. (Id. at p. 543.) The court notes that it will enforce contractual sanctions where the parties have agreed to such a sanction for failing to attempt to mediate. (See Cullen v. Corwin (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1074, 1079 [provision barring recovery of attorney's fees because party failed to mediate enforced].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS, INC. VS. MADE FOR RETAIL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS, INC.'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff the Center for Public Awareness, Inc.'s motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will sign the proposed order and consent judgment included in the moving papers. =(302/EPS)
